facts.text: Catskill Village’s Main Street is lined with historic shops and restaurants, a living reminder of the town’s 19th‑century commercial heart.
The Thomas Cole National Historic Site, perched on the Hudson River, offers views of the Catskill Mountains and a glimpse into America’s landscape‑painting roots.
A public boat launch called Dutchman’s Landing lets visitors paddle into the Hudson River, a favorite spot for fishing and kayaking.
Catskill is the setting of Washington Irving’s classic tale “Rip Van Winkle,” linking the village to American literary heritage.
The town’s first settler, Derrick Teunis van Vechten, built a house there in 1680, marking the beginning of Catskill’s long history.
Catskill Village welcomes visitors with a blend of historic architecture, river activities, and an Audubon nature preserve where bald eagles sometimes appear.
